retrieved_docs.title: T Test -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: William Sealy Gosset first described the t-test in 1908, when he published his article under the pseudonym 'student' while working for a brewery. [1] In simple terms, a Student's t-test is a ratio that quantifies how significant the difference is between the 'means' of 2 groups while considering their variance or distribution.

retrieved_docs.title: McNemar And Mann-Whitney U Tests -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Conventionally, statistical tests divide into two major groups, parametric and non-parametric. The prerequisite of using a parametric analysis is that the data tested assumes a normal (Gaussian) distribution. If the data is not in a normal distribution, non-parametric tests are used. There are many non-parametric tests analogous to parametric tests in continuous variables, namely Mann-Whitney U test and independent t-test, Wilcoxon signed-rank test, and paired t-test, Kruskal Wali's test and Analysis of Variance (ANOVA), and Spearman rank correlation coefficient and Pearson product-moment coefficient. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: McNemar And Mann-Whitney U Tests -- Definition/Introduction -- Mann Whitney U test
retrieved_docs.content: Mann Whitney U test or Wilcoxon Rank-Sum test, on the other hand, is an analog of the parametric Student's t-test. It compares the means between two independent groups with the assumption that the data is not in a normal distribution. Therefore, it is useful for numerical/continuous variables. For example, if researchers want to compare two different groups' age or height (continuous variables), in a study with non-normally distributed data, then the Mann Whitney U test can be used.

retrieved_docs.title: McNemar And Mann-Whitney U Tests -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Historically, statistical testing has been a grueling and labor-intensive process. Thanks to modernization and the use of computers, statistical analysis can now be accomplished through many commercially available programs, such as the Statistical Program for Social Sciences (SPSS) or Software for Statistics and Data Science (STATA).

retrieved_docs.title: McNemar And Mann-Whitney U Tests -- Definition/Introduction -- McNemar test
retrieved_docs.content: If the Chi-squared value is significant, the null hypothesis is rejected, meaning there is a substantial difference in the marginal proportions of the tests, i.e., the newer treatment/ diagnostic modalities/biomarker is a better alternative to prevalent practice.

retrieved_docs.title: McNemar And Mann-Whitney U Tests -- Definition/Introduction -- McNemar test
retrieved_docs.content: The McNemar test is a non-parametric test used to analyze paired nominal data. It is a test on a 2 x 2 contingency table and checks the marginal homogeneity of two dichotomous variables. The test requires one nominal variable with two categories (dichotomous) and one independent variable with two dependent groups. Also, the two groups in the dependent variable must be mutually exclusive, i.e., cannot be in more than one group. The minimal sample size required for the McNemar test is at least ten discordant pairs. The formula for calculating the Chi-squared value for McNemar test appears in Image 1, where b is the false positive count, and c is the false negative count.

retrieved_docs.title: McNemar And Mann-Whitney U Tests -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: All good research is based on a meticulous and well-designed question in the form of a hypothesis. To test this hypothesis, one must conduct an experiment with strict guidelines to obtain robust results. The results are then tested using statistics to examine its significance and conclude if a new treatment/ diagnostic modalities/biomarker is a better alternative to prevalent practice. Thus, statistical tests are an important component of research, especially in the fields of medicine.

retrieved_docs.title: McNemar And Mann-Whitney U Tests -- Definition/Introduction -- McNemar test
retrieved_docs.content: Therefore, others developed a more precise approach to deal with this situation. The McNemar mid- p test considerably improves the statistical significance without violating the nominal level. Furthermore, if small but frequent violations at the nominal level are acceptable, then the McNemar asymptotic test, is the most powerful test that for this purpose. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: McNemar And Mann-Whitney U Tests -- Definition/Introduction -- McNemar test
retrieved_docs.content: For nominal variables, in the form of a 2 x 2 table, three types of statistical tests can be used. The first one is the Fisher's exact test. The preconditions for its use are binary data and unpaired samples. The second one is the McNemar test, which requires binary data as in Fisher's exact, albeit with paired samples. The third one is the Chi-squared test, requiring a sample size of more than 60 subjects, with more than five counts in each cell. The Chi-squared test can also be useful for a contingency table of more than 2 x 2, i.e., 3 x 3, 4 x 4, and so on. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: McNemar And Mann-Whitney U Tests -- Definition/Introduction -- McNemar test
retrieved_docs.content: It should be noted that if the sum of discordant pairs (b+c) is small (<25), even if the total sample size is large, the statistical power of the McNemar test is low. Thus, in research studies with small sample size, and the sum of discordant pairs is less than 25, the exact binomial test can be used. Alternatively, Edwards's continuity correction is another option. [3] Nevertheless, the use of an exact test in studies with few subjects will produce unnecessary large p values with poor power.

retrieved_docs.title: T Test -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: These variations of the student's t-test use observed or collected data to calculate a test statistic, which can then be used to calculate a p-value. Often misinterpreted, the p-value is equal to the probability of collecting data that is at least as extreme as the observed data in the study, assuming that the null hypothesis is true. [3] Examples best illustrate this concept, as in the article's questions. Often, a threshold value is set before the study (equal to the alpha mentioned above); if the resulting p-value is below the preset threshold, there is sufficient evidence to reject the null hypothesis.

retrieved_docs.title: Types of Variables and Commonly Used Statistical Designs -- Issues of Concern -- Paired T-test
retrieved_docs.content: A paired t-test analyzes one sample population, measuring the same variable on two different occasions; this is often useful for intervention and educational research. Example : Does participating in a research curriculum (one group with intervention) improve resident performance on a test to measure research competence (continuous)?

retrieved_docs.title: McNemar And Mann-Whitney U Tests -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: There are several issues of concern regarding the use of the McNemar test and the Mann-Whitney U test, explained as follows:

retrieved_docs.title: T Test -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: Selecting appropriate statistical tests is a critical step in conducting research. [2] Therefore, there are 3 forms of Student’s t-test about which physicians, particularly physician-scientists, need to be aware: (1) 1-sample t-test, (2) 2-sample t-test, and (3) 2-sample paired t-test. The 1-sample t-test evaluates a single list of numbers to test the hypothesis that a statistic of that set is equal to a chosen value, for instance, to test the hypothesis that the mean of the set of numbers is equal to zero. For example, consider the following question: what is the average serum sodium concentration in adults? Currently, 140 mEq/L is an approximate center of a reference range of 135 to 145 mEq/L; thus, the null hypothesis is that the average serum sodium concentration in adults equals 140 mEq/L.

retrieved_docs.title: McNemar And Mann-Whitney U Tests -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: 1. McNemar test compares paired categorical data. However, it can not be used to measure an agreement because the McNemar test compares the overall proportion. For example, if a researcher is comparing the test results of subjects examined by two different persons, and the proportion of subjects who pass the test between these populations are the same, this cannot be concluded as evidence of an agreement. [5]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 3 -- Community 2021
retrieved_docs.content: The Bone Mineral Density Test is identified as a concept that is included within the CDISC SDTM Terminology, being part of the terminology’s subset of included concepts.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 4 -- Community 2867
retrieved_docs.content: Diagnostic physical therapy is classified as a test cesty, indicating that it functions as a specific type of test within that category.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 4 -- Community 2830
retrieved_docs.content: CDISC SDTM Terminology, a standardized framework for organizing clinical trial data, includes the concept “test cesty” as well as the concept “عدد خلايا الدم الحمراء” within its defined subset of terms, indicating that both items are recognized components of this terminology set.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2239
retrieved_docs.content: The electrocardiogram, a diagnostic test that records the heart’s electrical activity, is classified in Arabic as تخطيط كهربية القلب, reflecting its direct translation and terminology across languages.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 4 -- Community 2732
retrieved_docs.content: The Bone Mineral Density Test is recognized as a subset concept within the Clinical Data Interchange Standards Consortium Terminology, indicating that this diagnostic assessment is formally included in the standardized terminology used for clinical data exchange.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 4 -- Community 2902
retrieved_docs.content: The concept “Without” is associated with قيلة مستقيمية.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1826
retrieved_docs.content: The category “signs and symptoms of ill‑defined conditions” is presented as a parent concept that includes the specific child entity “غشي.” In this framework, the general term “Symptoms” is described as closely related to, and potentially synonymous with, the broader phrase “signs and symptoms of ill‑defined conditions,” indicating that the two expressions refer to overlapping clinical manifestations. Consequently, the specific manifestation “غشي” is understood to fall under the umbrella of nonspecific or ill‑defined symptomatology, and clinicians should recognize its inclusion within this broader symptom category when evaluating and documenting patient presentations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1955
retrieved_docs.content: In medical terminology, the phrase “Above reference range” signifies an increased value and is categorized as an “isa” relationship, indicating that it represents a condition in which a measured parameter exceeds its normal reference limits.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 4 -- Community 2896
retrieved_docs.content: Cardiac function is a broad physiological concept that includes measures such as معدل ضربات القلب (heart rate). In clinical practice, the evaluation of cardiac function is interpreted specifically in the context of congestive heart failure in patients who are non‑hypertensive, providing important information for diagnosis and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 4 -- Community 2846
retrieved_docs.content: The provided triples involve non‑medical terms and do not convey any medical concepts or relationships, so no medical summary can be generated from them.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2578
retrieved_docs.content: Acute and unspecified renal failure is described as being related to and possibly synonymous with كلاء الكليون السفلي, indicating that the two terms may refer to the same clinical concept.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 4 -- Community 2754
retrieved_docs.content: Essential hypertension, a common form of high blood pressure, is interpreted by the term ضغط الدم, the Arabic phrase that directly translates to “blood pressure,” highlighting the linguistic link between the clinical condition and its description.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 4 -- Community 2908
retrieved_docs.content: Other and ill-defined heart disease is synonymous with تمدد الأوعية الدموية في القلب, a term that describes dilation of the blood vessels within the heart. This equivalence indicates that the ambiguous classification of certain cardiac conditions corresponds to a pathological enlargement of the coronary or myocardial vasculature, which can affect cardiac function and warrants appropriate diagnostic evaluation and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1722
retrieved_docs.content: The concept “Without” is associated with both Arabic medical descriptors “قيلة مثانية” and “قيلة مستقيمية,” indicating a relationship between the state of being without and these two conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 1771
retrieved_docs.content: Symptoms are closely related to and may be considered synonymous with the broader category of signs and symptoms of ill‑defined conditions. In this hierarchical framework, diseases and injuries are classified as having the parent category of signs and symptoms of ill‑defined conditions, indicating that they are understood through the manifestation of such nonspecific signs. A specific example of a child element within this category is الانهيار في الدورة الدموية (circulatory collapse), which represents a distinct clinical manifestation that can arise from ill‑defined conditions. Together, these relationships highlight that the identification of symptoms and signs, including serious events like circulatory collapse, is essential for recognizing and managing diseases and injuries that present without clear, well‑defined diagnostic criteria.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2407
retrieved_docs.content: In obstetric care, the broad category “other procedures to assist delivery” encompasses a more specific subgroup called “Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery.” Within this subgroup, the Arabic‑named concept “فشل التوليد بالملقط” is identified as a child entity, indicating that it represents a particular type of procedure or complication related to assisted delivery. This hierarchical organization shows that “Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery” is a child of “other procedures to assist delivery,” and “فشل التوليد بالملقط” is a child of “Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery,” reflecting their interrelated roles in the classification of delivery‑assisting interventions.
